message.content:
You are confirmer #2, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-007 in scripts/capture_transcripts.py
What: transcript scrubber redacts the bare operator username everywhere (derived from home dir), not only in /Users|/home paths.
Test(s): test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

c49261e fix(hardening-p4): batch D — CI / supply-chain reproducibility (G-015,016,022)

0788961 docs(hardening): pass-3 merged (#161); open pass-4 with retrospective dimensions

---
commit 7dccfd8b5f8563c0da90d72ee92d954b99605422
Author: [REDACTED] <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 00:10:40 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
    
    Pass-4 confirmed findings (2-agent verified, revert-discriminated):
    
    - G-007 (medium) the transcript scrubber redacts the bare operator username everywhere
      (git author strings, project-dir slugs, prose), not only inside /Users|/home paths.
    - G-018 (low) the high-stakes egress denylist now scrubs the ENTIRE ANTHROPIC_* namespace
      (any endpoint/auth/header/custom-header var) plus NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS /
      NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED (custom-CA / cert-bypass MITM enablers).
    - G-019 (low) Gate.evaluate type-guards the precondition's return (non-GateDecision ->
      typed GateError), the gate analogue of the #F-021 Verdict guards.
    - G-020 (low) make_gate_node records to the trail before returning the state update and, on
      a recorder failure, raises a clear GateError instead of returning the update — so graph
      state can't diverge from the tamper-evident trail.
    - G-021 (low) build_baseline_artifact names the offending extra_input key when it isn't
      canonicalizable (was a raw, context-free ValueError/TypeError from hash_value).
    - G-024 (low) corrected the cosine_similarity_matrix_array docstring: the determinism freeze
      copies the array bytes once (O(size)), it is NOT zero-copy/O(1).
    - G-013 (medium) test-discrimination: the cosine fast-path test now asserts dispatch to the
      numpy path AND numpy==pure agreement (a revert to always-pure now fails).
    - G-014 (medium) test-discrimination: the SIGTERM flush test asserts the handler is installed
      unconditionally on the main thread (was gated on `if "h" in captured`, silently skippable).
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 520 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

diff --git a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
index 6c4362e..9192965 100755
--- a/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
@@ -52,6 +52,26 @@ _SECRET_PATTERNS = [
 ]
 
 
+def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
+    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
+
+    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
+    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
+    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
+    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
+    """
+    try:
+        name = Path.home().name
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
+        return None
+    if not name or len(name) < 4:
+        return None
+    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
+
+
+_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
+
+
 def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
     for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
         if pat.groups == 2:
@@ -60,6 +80,10 @@ def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
             text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
         else:
             text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
+    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
+    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
+    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
+        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
     return text

30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED] _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
56	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
57	
58	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
59	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-[REDACTED]-...``). Derive the
60	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
61	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
62	    """
63	    try:
64	        name = Path.home().name
65	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
66	        return None
67	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
68	        return None
69	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
70	
71	
72	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
73	
74	
75	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
76	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
77	        if pat.groups == 2:
78	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
79	        elif pat.groups == 1:
80	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
81	        else:
82	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
83	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
84	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
85	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
86	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
87	    return text
88	
89	
90	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
91	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
92	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
93	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
94	
95	
96	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
97	    try:
98	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
99	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()

1	"""Guards for repo scripts + examples (pass 3: #F-051, #F-048).
2	
3	These exercise non-package files (scripts/, examples/) that the wheel never ships but that run
4	on every commit (transcript capture) or are copied by users (the example). Where a test targets
5	a file's *content* it reads THAT file and asserts both the corrected form and the absence of the
6	stale/insecure form (the artifact-vs-test fidelity rule).
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import importlib.util
12	from pathlib import Path
13	
14	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
15	
16	
17	def _load(rel: str, name: str):
18	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(name, _ROOT / rel)
19	    assert spec and spec.loader
20	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
21	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
22	    return mod
23	
24	
25	def test_bearer_token_scrub_covers_base64_chars():
26	    # #F-051: a bearer token containing base64 chars (+ / =) must be FULLY redacted, not split at
27	    # the first such char leaving a trailing fragment in the transcript.
28	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts")
29	    text = "Authorization: [REDACTED]"
30	    scrubbed = cap._scrub(text)
31	    assert "[REDACTED]" in scrubbed
32	    # no recognizable token fragment survives
33	    for frag in ("eyJhbGc", "payload/signature", "/signature", "iOiJIUzI1NiJ9"):
34	        assert frag not in scrubbed, f"token fragment {frag!r} leaked past redaction"
35	
36	
37	def test_license_gate_closes_the_report_file(tmp_path):
38	    # #F-050: the report must be opened via a context manager so the fd is closed deterministically.
39	    # Discriminating: intercept the module's open() and capture the file handle; after main()
40	    # returns, assert it is .closed. A bare json.load(open(...)) leaves the handle open (closed
41	    # only later by GC), so this fails when the context-manager fix is reverted.
42	    import builtins
43	    import json
44	
45	    gate = _load("scripts/license_gate.py", "license_gate_fd")
46	    p = tmp_path / "licenses.json"
47	    p.write_text(json.dumps([{"Name": "ok", "Version": "1", "License": "MIT"}]), encoding="utf-8")
48	
49	    handles = []
50	    real_open = builtins.open
51	
52	    def tracking_open(*a, **k):
53	        fh = real_open(*a, **k)
54	        handles.append(fh)
55	        return fh
56	
57	    gate.open = tracking_open  # module globals win over builtins for the bare open() call
58	    rc = gate.main(str(p))
59	    assert rc == 0
60	    assert handles and all(h.closed for h in handles), (
61	        "license_gate left the report file open (#F-050)"
62	    )
63	
64	
65	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_operator_pii():
66	    # #F-024 (mandate-compatible mitigation): transcripts are committed as provenance per the
67	    # campaign mandate, but the capture scrubber must mask operator PII — email addresses and the
68	    # home-path username — so identity is not published. The path STRUCTURE is preserved.
69	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_pii")
70	    out = cap._scrub("user [REDACTED] at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py and /home/[REDACTED]/y")
71	    assert "[REDACTED]" not in out
72	    assert "somebody" not in out
73	    assert "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/x.py" in out  # structure kept, username masked
74	    assert "/home/[REDACTED]/y" in out
75	
76	
77	def test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username():
78	    # #G-007: the operator username must be redacted wherever it appears (git author strings,
79	    # project-dir slugs like -Users-[REDACTED]-..., prose), not only inside /Users|/home paths.
80	    from pathlib import Path
81	
82	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_user")
83	    name = Path.home().name
84	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
85	        import pytest
86	
87	        pytest.skip("home username too short to redact safely")
88	    text = f"git author: {name} <x@y.z>; project dir -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
89	    out = cap._scrub(text)
90	    assert name not in out  # bare handle masked everywhere, not only in a /Users path
91	
92	
93	def test_example_uses_no_hardcoded_hmac_secret():
94	    # #F-048: the example must not carry a shippable, publicly-known HMAC secret a user could copy
95	    # into production. It derives a fresh random secret per run instead.
96	    src = (_ROOT / "examples" / "minimal_study.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
97	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
98	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place
99

content.text:
The test discriminates: it fails when the fix is reverted, passes with it. File restored (diff stat empty). Let me verify no regression and check the edge-case concern about `len < 4` and over-redaction.

The fix derives the username from `Path.home().name`, escapes it, and redacts as a standalone token after the path rule. Concerns to assess:

1. **Substring over-redaction**: The pattern is a bare `re.escape(name)` with no word boundaries — so a username appearing as a substring of an unrelated word gets redacted. This is intentional (the slug `-Users-[REDACTED]-` and `<name>/path` need substring matching), and it's conservative (over-redact rather than leak). The `len < 4` guard prevents catastrophic over-redaction for short handles. Acceptable for a defense scrubber.

2. **No regression**: existing `test_transcript_scrub_redacts_operator_pii` still passes (verified, 5 passed). The path rule runs first, then username sub catches stragglers — no conflict.

3. **`Path.home()` exception guarded** — returns None, scrubber no-ops on username (path rule still active). Robust.

The test mirrors the real leak vectors named in G-007 (git author string, `-Users-[REDACTED]-` slug) and asserts the bare handle is absent.

input.reasoning:
Fix verified in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py (lines 55-87). A new _username_pattern() derives the operator handle from Path.home().name, re.escape()s it, and _scrub() applies _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text) AFTER the existing _SECRET_PATTERNS loop (so the /Users|/home path rule runs first and the username sub catches stragglers in git-author strings, prose, and -Users-[REDACTED]- project-dir slugs). This precisely matches the G-007 description.

Appropriate: idiomatic (compiled once at module load), defensive (try/except around Path.home() returns None so capture never breaks), and conservative — skips handles shorter than 4 chars / empty (e.g. CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words. Ordering is correct so masked paths aren't re-exposed. No regression: the pre-existing test_transcript_scrub_redacts_operator_pii (path-structure preservation) still passes; full file suite 5 passed.

Complete + discriminating: I reverted the fix (removed the two-line username-sub block) and ran tests/test_scripts.py::test_transcript_scrub_redacts_bare_operator_username — it FAILED ('[REDACTED]' contained in 'git author: [REDACTED] ... -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc'). With the fix restored it PASSES. The test exercises both named leak vectors (git author string and the -Users-[REDACTED]- slug) and asserts the bare handle is absent everywhere, and it self-skips when the home handle is <4 chars (consistent with the production guard). File was restored; git diff --stat clean.

One non-blocking note: the redaction is a bare escaped substring (no word boundaries), so a username that is a substring of an unrelated word would also be masked — but this is intentional and required to catch the slug/path-fragment cases, and over-redaction is the safe direction for a PII scrubber. Verdict CONFIRMED.

content.text: Verified G-007 as CONFIRMED. The fix correctly redacts the bare operator username everywhere via a home-derived escaped pattern applied after the path rule, and the test discriminates (fails on revert, passes with fix), covers both named leak vectors, and introduces no regression.
